title: Page Layout — CSS Grid
description: Create a two-column page layout using CSS Grid. Add a .page-grid class with display: grid and grid-template-columns to define a main content area and a sidebar. Style the .sidebar with padding and a background color. Use grid-gap (or gap) to add spacing between the columns.
test_code:
assert 'display' in css.lower() and 'grid' in css.lower(), 'Missing display: grid on .page-grid'
assert 'grid-template-columns' in css.lower(), 'Missing grid-template-columns'
assert '.sidebar' in css.lower(), 'Missing .sidebar selector'
assert 'grid-gap' in css.lower() or 'gap' in css.lower(), 'Missing grid-gap or gap for spacing between columns'
assert '.page-grid' in css.lower(), 'Missing .page-grid selector'
acceptance_criteria: .page-grid uses display: grid with grid-template-columns for a two-column layout
.sidebar has padding and a background color
grid-gap or gap adds spacing between the columns
hints: grid-template-columns: 2fr 1fr creates a main area twice as wide as the sidebar
grid-gap: 30px adds consistent spacing between grid cells
The sidebar could have a light background like #f0f0f0 to visually separate it
